title: Primula mathildae
intro: Primula mathildae, commonly known as Mathilda's primrose or Mathilda's primula, is a species of flowering plant in the family Primulaceae, characterized by its delicate, showy flowers and fragrant foliage. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments, including skin conditions and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Primula mathildae include primulin, a yellow pigment with potential antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, as well as other flavonoids and phenolic acids. Traditional herbal preparations made with Primula mathildae include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support skin health and immune function.
